content.text: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote theorems (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the terms Euclidean domain and principal ideal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that in a Euclidean domain every ideal is a principal ideal.

problem.text: Let \(p\) be a prime and \(R=\{r\in\mathbb{Q}\mid r=\frac{a}{b}\text{ for some }a,b\in\mathbb{Z}\text{ with }p\nmid b\}\). Prove that \(pR\) is the unique maximal ideal of \(R\).

Using the rational canonical form, find one representative for each conjugacy class in \(\operatorname{GL}(4,\mathbb{F}_2)\) whose elements have order \(3\) or \(6\). (Hint: in \(\mathbb{F}_2[x]\) we have \(x^6-1=(x^3-1)^2\).)
Determine which of the following are irreducible in the given rings.
Let \(F\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(F\)-vector space and \(T\in\operatorname{End}_F(V)\) a linear operator.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Explain why there is an \(F\)-algebra homomorphism \(\psi_T:F[x]\to\operatorname{End}_F(V)\) mapping \(x\) to \(T\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Let \(F[T]\) be the \(F\)-subalgebra of \(\operatorname{End}_F(V)\) generated by \(T\). Prove that if \(m_T(x)\) is irreducible in \(F[x]\) then every nonzero element of \(F[T]\) is invertible (under composition of operators).

problem.text: Let \(n\geq 1\) and let \(F=\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{1},\sqrt{2},\ldots,\sqrt{n})\). Show that \(\sqrt[3]{2}\notin F\).
